questions.q: Which kubectl command will output the image name of the first container running in a pod named `web-pod`?
options: kubectl describe pod web-pod | grep image
questions.explanation: The image definition is stored in the pod specification under `spec.containers`. Since it's an array, you use `[0]` to access the first container, followed by `.image` to get the image string.

questions.q: You need to extract the base64-encoded value of a key named `db-password` from a Secret named `my-secret`. Which JSONPath expression is correct?
questions.explanation: Secret contents are stored in the `.data` dictionary of the API object. To access a specific key, you use `{.data.<key-name>}`. You can then pipe this output to `base64 -d` to decode it.

questions.q: Which JSONPath syntax is used to iterate over a list of items and format them with newlines?
questions.explanation: JSONPath in kubectl uses the `{range <array>}` and `{end}` syntax to loop through items, allowing you to print text, variables, tabs `{"\t"}`, and newlines `{"\n"}` for each item in the array.
